steps.title: Identify what directly supports the hypothesis
steps.content: The researchers claim PCFG whales are smaller as an adaptation to “distinct resource opportunities.” Direct support requires both showing what those opportunities are and explaining why smaller size would be advantageous.

steps.title: Find where size is actually adaptive
steps.content:
Rocky kelp beds at shallow depths (choice C) are inaccessible to larger whales, making smaller body size a genuine advantage in that habitat. A focuses on human proximity rather than foraging resources, B merely confirms the size pattern exists without explaining any adaptive advantage, and D identifies different prey without showing why being smaller would be necessary. Being small allows access to shallow kelp beds—that directly explains the hypothesized adaptation.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
